NCT04792489,1,"Age range allowed: 18 years and older. Sex allowed: All sexes. Cancer type allowed: Diffuse large B‑cell lymphoma. Histology allowed: Diffuse large B‑cell lymphoma not otherwise specified or any associated subtype—including high‑grade B‑cell lymphoma with MYC and BCL2 and/or BCL6 rearrangements, high‑grade B‑cell lymphoma not otherwise specified, primary mediastinal (thymic) large B‑cell lymphoma, or transformed lymphoma such as transformed follicular lymphoma, transformed marginal zone lymphoma, or follicular lymphoma grade 3. Cancer burden allowed: Relapsed or refractory disease after failure of at least two lines of systemic therapy that each contained an anti‑CD20 monoclonal antibody and an anthracenedione/anthracycline regimen, and either progression after autologous stem cell transplant, ineligibility for transplant, or decision not to undergo transplant. column1: NCT06056323,1,"Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: pancreatic cancer. Histology allowed: pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: unresectable, locally advanced, recurrent, or metastatic disease. Biomarkers excluded: NA.","• Presence of a second malignancy diagnosed < 5 years ago, except appropriately treated superficial skin cancers, in‑situ cervix/breast lesions, localized prostate cancer, or T1 urothelial carcinoma.
• Clinically active central nervous system metastases or leptomeningeal disease (patients with previously treated, stable, asymptomatic CNS involvement may be eligible).

• Recent major cardiovascular events (myocardial infarction, stroke, transient ischemic attack, unstable angina) or New York Heart Association class III–IV congestive heart failure occurring within six months preceding enrollment.
• Baseline electrocardiogram showing QT interval corrected > 480 milliseconds or uncontrolled ventricular/tachyarrhythmia.

• Current active autoimmune disorder necessitating systemic immunosuppression within the past two years (exceptions: well‑controlled thyroid autoimmunity, vitiligo, Graves’ disease, Hashimoto’s thyroiditis, type Ⅰ diabetes, remote/asymptomatic asthma or atopic disease).
• Previous allogeneic stem‑cell transplantation or solid‑organ transplantation.
• Documented severe IgE‑mediated or grade 3–4 hypersensitivity reaction to monoclonal antibodies, recombinant proteins, or any excipient contained in the investigational product.
• History of grade 3–4 immune‑related adverse events leading to permanent cessation of prior therapy (exception made for hormonally replaceable endocrine toxicities).

• Known chronic viral infections posing heightened risk: untreated active hepatitis B (viral load ≥ 1000 copies/ml), unresolved hepatitis C replication, active tuberculosis currently being treated, or ongoing anti‑tb regimen initiated within the past year.
• Human immunodeficiency virus infection with CD4⁺ lymphocyte count < 350 cells/mm³ (subjects with higher counts may enrol).

• Pregnancy, lactation, or unwillingness/unavailability of reliable contraception for women of child‑bearing potential throughout treatment and for the stipulated post‑study observation period.",NCT06056323-1

column1: NCT05563220,1,"Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Both males and females. Cancer type allowed: Breast cancer. column1: NCT04841148,1,"Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Invasive breast cancer. Histology allowed: Invasive carcinoma (ductal or lobular) that is Estrogen Receptor positive AND Human epidermal growth factor Receptor 2 negative. Cancer burden allowed: Stage II or Stage III disease with no clinical or imaging evidence of locoregional recurrence or distant metastasis
column2: must have detectable disseminated tumor cells in bone marrow by immunohistochemistry. Prior treatment required: Definitive surgical removal of the breast tumor PLUS completion of planned postoperative chemotherapy and radiation therapy
column3: currently receiving adjuvant endocrine therapy (an aromatase inhibitor) at time of enrollment
column4: may have received a cyclin-dependent kinase 4/6 inhibitor other than palbociclib (discontinued ≥6 months before screening)
column5: may be receiving approved bone‐modifying agents (bisphosphonate or RANK ligand inhibitor). Prior treatment excluded: Participation in another interventional clinical trial
column6: concurrent tamoxifen use when randomized to a hydroxychloroquine‑containing arm
column7: any adjuvant endocrine therapy other than an aromatase inhibitor (including tamoxifen, fulvestrant, experimental hormonal agents). Biomarkers required: Detection of disseminated tumor cells in bone marrow by immunohistochemistry (screening assay)
column8: Estrogen Receptor positive AND HER2 negative status on the primary tumor (assessment at screening). Biomarkers excluded: NA.","History of another prior invasive breast cancer (except DCIS more than five years earlier).

column1: Other prior invasive malignancies requiring disease‑free interval of fewer than five years.
Chronic high‑dose systemic corticosteroid use exceeding predefined thresholds.
QTc prolongation greater than 480 milliseconds.
Uncontrolled cardiac arrhythmia (excluding well‑controlled atrial fibrillation).
Recent (< six months) acute coronary syndrome, coronary intervention, or stent placement.
Class II or higher congestive heart failure (NYHA classification).
Past or existing pneumonitis/interstitial lung disease or DLCO < 50 percent predicted or resting oxygen saturation ≤ 88 %.
Uncontrolled diabetes mellitus.
Active serious infection or untreated opportunistic infection.
Significant hepatic impairment (cirrhosis, chronic active hepatitis).
HIV infection requiring antiretroviral therapy (due to PK interaction concerns).
Severe gastrointestinal disorders affecting oral drug absorption.
Bleeding diatheses or therapeutic anticoagulants use.
Retinal pathology such as retinitis pigmentosa or recent retinal vascular occlusion.
Pregnancy, breastfeeding, or lack of adequate contraception in persons of reproductive potential.",NCT04841148-1

column1: NCT06569147,1,"Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Primary systemic light chain (AL) amyloidosis. Histology allowed: Light chain amyloid deposition disease. Cancer burden allowed: Relapsed or refractory disease after ≥1 prior line of therapy

column4: refractory to belantamab mafodotin
column5: receipt of belantamab mafodotin as the immediate prior line of therapy. column6: assessments performed at screening. Biomarkers excluded: NA.","History of allergic reaction to elranatamab. Current enrollment in another interventional study for AL amyloidosis. Pregnancy, lactation, or intention to conceive during study and up to four months post‑treatment. Lack of effective contraception for women of child‑bearing potential and men. Unable to comprehend consent or comply with study procedures. Concurrent active malignancy excluding adequately treated skin cancers, in‑situ cervical cancer, stage I solid tumors in remission >2 years, or low‑risk prostate cancer (Gleason <7, PSA <10 ng/mL). Fulfillment of Multiple Myeloma CRAB criteria (active MM). Clinically significant untreated autoimmune disease. Positive HIV serology. Uncontrolled Hepatitis B or C infection (must be suppressed/cured). Severe uncontrolled orthostatic hypotension causing syncope/presyncope. History of sustained ventricular tachycardia, aborted ventricular fibrillation, or symptomatic AV/Nodal bradyarrhythmia without pacing device. Corrected QT interval >550 msec absent a paced rhythm. Acute myocardial ischemia or concerning conduction disturbances on screening electrocardiogram beyond isolated first-degree AV block, Mobitz I second-degree AV block, right or left bundle branch blocks, controlled atrial fibrillation, or stable bifascicular block deemed benign. Major surgical procedure under general anesthesia within four weeks before screening or anticipated during study. New York Heart Association class IV symptoms or acutely decompensated congestive heart failure. Ongoing investigation with other experimental agents for AL amyloidosis. Poor performance status exceeding ECOG 2/Karnofsky <60 %. Significant unresolved toxicity greater than grade 1 from prior anticancer therapy (aside from alopecia).",NCT06569147-1

column2: documented disease progression on prior standard therapy. Prior treatment excluded: Prior KRAS G12D inhibitor. Biomarkers required: KRAS G12D mutation detected (screening assay). History of organ transplantation, including allogenic hematopoietic stem‑cell transplantation. Clinically significant, untreated cardiac conditions (e.g., recent myocardial infarction, unstable angina, congestive heart failure). Uncontrolled hypertension or arrhythmias. Baseline electrocardiogram showing clinically significant abnormalities. Laboratory evidence of severe hepatic impairment, renal insufficiency, or bone marrow suppression beyond protocol-specified thresholds. Active infection requiring intravenous antibiotics, uncontrolled central nervous system involvement, or symptomatic brain metastases. Positive serologic tests for human immunodeficiency virus, active hepatitis B or C infections. Pregnancy, lactation, or unwillingness to use adequate contraception. Performance status > 1 (Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group score greater than 1).",NCT06179160-3

NCT04928677,1,Age range allowed: 12 months to < 22 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Primary extracranial solid tumor. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: Measurable disease per RECIST 1.1
column2: disease must be recurrent or refractory (tumor progressed despite maximal standard therapies or recurred within five years of completing planned therapy). Prior treatment required: Receipt of standard therapy resulting in recurrent or refractory disease. Prior treatment excluded: Prior GPC3‑directed immunotherapy
column3: concomitant anti‑cancer therapy or investigational agents. Biomarkers required: GPC3 positivity (minimum 1⁺ staining by immunohistochemistry) confirmed centrally during screening. Biomarkers excluded: NA.,"Patients receiving current anti‑cancer therapy or investigational agents are not eligible. Patients who do not have tumor tissue available for GPC3 testing are not eligible. Patients with uncontrolled seizures are not eligible. Patients requiring systemic corticosteroids (>0.15 mg/kg/day prednisolone equivalent) or other immunosuppressants (unless inhaled/topical or low-dose adrenal replacement) within 14 days are not eligible. Patients with a documented central nervous system tumor, CNS metastasis, CNS ischemia, or cerebral infarction are excluded. Baseline corrected QTc greater than 480 ms excludes participation. Individuals unable to attend scheduled follow‑ups, undergo requisite assessments, or meet safety monitoring obligations are excluded. Those with uncontrolled infections are excluded. Persons who have undergone prior solid organ transplantation are excluded.",NCT04928677-1

column1: NCT05372614,1,Age range allowed: 18 years and older. Sex allowed: both sexes. Cancer type allowed: breast cancer. Histology allowed: malignant neoplasm of the breast (all subtypes). Significant cardiovascular disease: myocardial infarction within six months, symptomatic congestive heart failure NYHA Class II–IV, left ventricular ejection fraction less than 50%, or resting QTc >470 ms (female) or >450 ms (male). Chronic gastrointestinal diseases causing Grade ≥ 2 diarrhoea at baseline. Presence of draining pleural, pericardial, or abdominal fluid requiring intervention. Spinal cord compression. Use of prohibited concomitant antitumour therapies (chemotherapy, targeted agents, immune checkpoint inhibitors, other ADCs) outside stipulated washout periods.
